rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALLEN J. CHUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine on the following issues as a supplement is pending for e-filing: (1) Notice of Hearing to Erica Chung should meet the 15-day requirement and be directly given to her in addition to her counsel. CRC 7.51(a)(b). Notice of Directions for Remote Appearance must be given. LRSF. 14.1.C.6). (2) Disbursement to Silke Finnis $5,000 for correction of erroneous deposit (Disbursement Schedule page 106). What deposit? Nothing in the receipt dated 6/22/17. (3) Caregiving expenses paid for March to April in 2016, totaling $4,650 (Disbursement Schedule page 105) (4) Note: It looks like fees paid to professionals for preparation of the court accounting and taxes. The amount may be deducted from the statutory fee. LR14.51 (Disbursement Schedule page 107) (5) Proof of service of Supplement (pending for e-filing 6/13/24). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For The Court To Put The Gaetano (Thomas) Iveli Revocable Trust Dated: December 9, 2022 Under Court Supervision
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. a) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner, despite this issue having been posted in the tentative ruling twice now. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] b) Notice appears not to have been given to the executor, only in care of his attorneys, contrary to CRC 7.51(a) and (b) and Probate Code Section 1214. c) The petition does not cite any legal authority for the Court to grant the relief. d) The creditors' joinder e-filed on 6/6/24 cites Probate Code Section 6405.2 in a footnote on page 6; however, there is no such Code section. If Section 6402.5 was meant, that chapter (Sections 6400 - 6414) concerns intestate estates and does not apply here. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF KEVIN KA-CHEUNG YU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. APPEARANCE FOR NO RESPONSE: If a hearing is continued for issues raised by an examiner to be addressed and there is no response prior to the continued hearing date, the matter is automatically continued again, usually for one month. If there is still no response, an appearance is required. If no one appears after the automatic second continuance, the matter is taken off calendar if it is not a compulsory petition, or an Order to Appear is issued. Issues were posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 4/8/24. There was no response before the 5/3/24 deadline, so the hearing was continued to this date. The issues still have not been addressed; the deadline was 6/7/24. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHARLES BOBB
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/17/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Post-deceased heir Rodrick Bobb Sr., reportedly had an estate plan that left his estate to his wife. No proof has been filed. No affidavit under Probate Code Section 13100 has been filed by her or whoever claims to be the successor to his interest. Distribution will not be to an "estate." (2) The assignment signed by James Robert Bob, Jr., was for $9,900 to Inheritance Funding Company, not $5,900. (3) The administrator does not even describe what comprised the purported costs totaling $207 for which she has no receipts but seeks reimbursement. They cannot be reimbursed without supporting documentation. (4) Is she not seeking reimbursement for the $380 she paid to the Louisiana attorneys? (5) One of the attorney's costs on 3/9/24 for certified copies of Letters appears to be a duplicate. (6) Why was it necessary to pay postage for "I&A" in 25 env? There is no statutory requirement that the I&A be served. (7) Notice was mailed to several heirs at addresses other than the addresses alleged in the petition to be theirs. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/5/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (There was no need to list the title of each assignment or to set forth all the terms of each assignment, especially when such terms had expired as to when rebates could be due.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHLEEN MURPHY IGNACIO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as counsel has not responded to the assigned examiner's e-mail (sent 06/04/2024) reminding counsel of this status hearing and asking whether counsel would need a continuance. Aside from the administrator's failure to petition for final distribution or for approval of a status report in a timely fashion, the Court notes the following issues: 1) Christopher Ignacio, identified in the closed conservatorship case as the decedent (conservatee)'s child "who has autism and is institutionalized somewhere in the east bay", was not disclosed in the petition to open this probate. 2) Christopher does not appear to have received notice of any kind of this probate, and no application has been made to prescribe or dispense with notice or to appoint a guardian ad litem. 3) If Christopher lacks legal capacity, he may need a conservator of the estate to receive distribution of his 1/3 share of this estate. 4) Benny Ignacio, the decedent's surviving (separated) spouse, has not made a Probate Code section 13502 election to probate his 1/3 share of this estate. Unless the administrator files a section 13502.5 petition to compel inclusion of Benny's fractional share (otherwise passing to him without probate), the inventory and appraisal must be corrected to remove the value of his share. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AURORA SAMSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) It is unclear whether Petitioner is arguing that Probate Code 6124 does not apply or is attempting to rebut the presumption under 6124. 2) Petitioner requests admission of lost will dated 10/8/00, which is not self-proving, and for which the witnesses are deceased. 3) Petitioner requests admission of the lost "codicil" dated January 2016, which appears to be a standalone instrument, not a codicil. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE OW 1996 FAMILY TRUST DATED 5/24/96 AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For (1) Order Confirming Real Property To Trust; (2) Order Conveying Personal Property To Trust And For 859 Damages; (3) Cancellatio
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/31/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it a printout of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The notice at item 1 must show the complete title of the pleading to be heard. (4) A courtesy copy of the petition and related papers were apparently not delivered to the Court as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. (none was received by the assigned examiner), so it was only cursorily reviewed online but it appears that the petitioner/trustee resides in San Mateo. (5) Notice was not given to Yuk Wah Yip Cho, a presumptive contingent beneficiary of the 2017 trust. Is she not entitled to notice? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of to the assigned examiner by 7/19/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CELESTE A BLANKOWITCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural issues with Daniel Leal's petition: 1) Petition is incomplete. Item 5a box (5) or (6) must be checked, and box (7) or (8) must be checked. Relationship to decedent must be indicated for all persons listen in item 8. 2) No Duties and Liabilities has been filed (Probate Code Section 8404). 3) No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Form DE-147S) has been filed (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.E). 4) No proof of publication has been filed (Probate Code Sections 8120 and 8121). 5) No Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (Form DE-121) has been filed (Probate Code Section 8100). All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 6) No proposed order has been received (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HELEN A. MCQUILLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/3/24. The following issues to be cleared a week before the hearing: Continued to 7/8/2024 1. Proposed Order for Probate to include a copy of the lost will offered to probate. Prob. Code sec. 8223 and LRSF 14.20. 2. An additional hard copy of the lost will must be provided to the clerk for a will file to be created before the hearing to admit the lost will to probate. LRSF 14.20. 3. Petition #8 to set forth the date of death of the deceased) spouse along with his or her name. 4. Petition #8 to identify the name of the parent of each nephew or niece so that the court may be able to review the completeness of notice. 5. Also, Petition #8 to clarify if any of the persons listed thereon is the trust beneficiary. Petitioner is the residuary beneficiary of the trust; however, it is not clear if there are other (specific) beneficiaries of the trust. 6. Petition needs to allege further for the court to review if Prob. Code sec. 6124 presumption has been overcome or not. Petition merely alleges that petitioner is unable to locate the original will. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
